user_query:
I’m trying to make our recurring "multi‑file doc update" workflow more reliable without rewriting everything each time. Please read the evidence and preferences in:
- input/friction_log.jsonl
- input/integration_prefs.yaml

Then set up a disciplined, one‑lever‑at‑a‑time improvement loop using only what’s in those files, and produce the following artifacts under output/:

1) output/local_notes_notice.txt — Before anything else, write a one‑line notice that this workflow will keep concise local notes for repeat improvement (so it’s clear we’ll persist lightweight notes for reuse).

2) output/experiments.md — Create a clean experiment log with one entry per experiment from input/friction_log.jsonl using a consistent template. Each entry must include these exact fields in order: Trigger, Baseline, Mutation, Outcome (better | worse | mixed), Evidence, Next move (promote | retest | discard). Only change one lever per experiment. If a mutation has three comparable wins in the evidence, set Next move to promote for that mutation; if an experiment proved worse, set Next move to discard; otherwise retest.

3) output/archive/retired-YYYYMMDD.md — If any experiment’s Next move is discard, create this archive note explaining what was tried, why it’s retired, and what we’ll avoid going forward. Place it in output/archive/ (create the folder if needed).

4) output/memory.md — Promote any proven pattern (three comparable wins) into a short, actionable rule under a "Stable Winners" section. Also include Status, Context (derived from input/integration_prefs.yaml), Guardrails (must include at least: "Never modify the installed skill itself" and "Never promote a rule after a single success"), and an updated date. Keep the rule concise and ready for future reuse.

5) output/summary.json — Provide a small machine‑readable summary for the promoted mutation: { "promoted_mutation": string, "runs_count": number, "average_speed_delta_sec": number (negative means faster), "total_error_reduction": number }. Base these on the comparable entries in input/friction_log.jsonl.

Notes:
- Do not invent experiments that aren’t in the log; normalize what’s there and make the minimal promotion/retirement decisions required by the value gate in the preferences.
- Only promote after three comparable wins; do not treat silence as proof.
- Keep all outputs in English and write only under output/ using relative paths.
